frameworks.name: spiking recurrent network model
frameworks.one_line: strongly-coupled spiking network trained on a subset of neurons to match cortical activity

frameworks.name: strongly-coupled cortical dynamics
frameworks.one_line: task-independent strong synapses set network state and spread activity

frameworks.name: distributed neural representation
frameworks.one_line: task variables are represented broadly across cortical populations

core_question: How does task-related activity become distributed across a cortical network when only a subset of neurons is plastic?
key_assumptions: cortex operates in a strongly-coupled dynamical regime
only a subset of neurons need be plastic to reproduce observed activity
synaptic interactions are strong enough to propagate task signals
manipulations: training subset of neurons to reproduce motor cortex activity during decision-making
key_findings: task-related activity emerged in untrained neurons across the network
strong untrained synapses mediated spread of task activity
untrained synapses determined the network's dynamical state
optogenetic perturbations support strongly-coupled motor cortex
interpretations: task-independent strong synapses distribute task representations from plastic subset to whole network
strong coupling is a cortical mechanism enabling distributed task-variable encoding
